behavior_in_task_description.reason: Instruction names the file path, env var name PLAYWRIGHT_SKIP_BROWSER_DOWNLOAD, getAsBooleanFromENV import path, resolveBrowsers with {shell:'no'}, ffmpeg, and the chromium fallback pattern.

no_solution_leakage.reason: Instruction explicitly dictates the implementation: use getAsBooleanFromENV from '../../server/utils/env', call browserRegistry.resolveBrowsers with {shell:'no'}, factor into a shared async helper taking name/channel string, and install ffmpeg — essentially describing the patch.

solution_uniqueness_guard.reason: Tests assert the exact helper name `async function resolveAndInstall(nameOrChannel: string)` and literal `await resolveAndInstall('ffmpeg')`/`await resolveAndInstall('chromium')`, which rejects correct alternatives with different helper names or signatures.

tests_verify_behavior_not_text.reason: In addition to text asserts, tests run npm build, eslint, lint-packages, check-deps as subprocesses.

test_not_tautological.reason: Tests require specific new code (env var check, resolveAndInstall helper, ffmpeg install call) plus a successful build; a stub returning nothing would fail build and string checks.

behavior_in_tests.reason: Env var check, helper with resolveBrowsers shell:'no', ffmpeg install, and chromium usage via helper are all tested.

informative_test_structure.reason: Tests are grouped with comment section headers (Gates, Fail-to-pass, Pass-to-pass, repo_tests).

instruction_no_hint_leakage.reason: Instruction provides near line-level guidance: 'At the very start of ensureConfiguredBrowserInstalled()', exact import path '../../server/utils/env', exact option {shell:'no'}, and directs to factor into a shared helper accepting name/channel — essentially the patch recipe.
